title: Checking into a Hotel
description: Practise checking in at the front desk, asking about facilities and handling a room issue.

transcript.speaker: receptionist
transcript.text: Good evening! Welcome to the Grand Plaza Hotel. Do you have a reservation?
transcript.translation: Bonsoir ! Bienvenue au Grand Plaza Hotel. Avez-vous une réservation ?

transcript.speaker: guest
transcript.text: Yes, I do. The booking is under the name Sarah Collins.
transcript.translation: Oui, j'en ai une. La réservation est au nom de Sarah Collins.

transcript.speaker: receptionist
transcript.text: Let me pull that up for you. Here we are — a superior double room, checking out on Friday. Is that correct?
transcript.translation: Je vais chercher ça pour vous. Voilà — une chambre double supérieure, départ vendredi. C'est bien ça ?

transcript.speaker: guest
transcript.text: Yes, that's right. Could I possibly get a room on a higher floor? I love the view.
transcript.translation: Oui, c'est exact. Serait-il possible d'avoir une chambre à un étage supérieur ? J'adore la vue.

transcript.speaker: receptionist
transcript.text: Absolutely. I'll upgrade you to the fourteenth floor at no extra charge. You'll have a lovely view of the river.
transcript.translation: Absolument. Je vais vous surclasser au quatorzième étage sans frais supplémentaires. Vous aurez une belle vue sur la rivière.

transcript.speaker: guest
transcript.text: That's wonderful, thank you! Does the room have Wi-Fi?
transcript.translation: C'est formidable, merci ! Est-ce que la chambre a le Wi-Fi ?

transcript.speaker: receptionist
transcript.text: Yes, complimentary high-speed Wi-Fi is available throughout the hotel. The password is on the welcome card in your room.
transcript.translation: Oui, le Wi-Fi haut débit est gratuit dans tout l'hôtel. Le mot de passe se trouve sur la carte de bienvenue dans votre chambre.

transcript.speaker: guest
transcript.text: Perfect. And what time is breakfast served?
transcript.translation: Parfait. Et à quelle heure est servi le petit-déjeuner ?

transcript.speaker: receptionist
transcript.text: Breakfast is served in the restaurant on the ground floor from six-thirty to ten-thirty. It's a full buffet.
transcript.translation: Le petit-déjeuner est servi au restaurant au rez-de-chaussée de six heures et demie à dix heures et demie. C'est un buffet complet.

transcript.speaker: guest
transcript.text: Brilliant. One last thing — I noticed a strange noise coming from the air conditioning when I walked past a room earlier. Is that something to be aware of?
transcript.translation: Parfait. Une dernière chose — j'ai remarqué un bruit étrange venant de la climatisation en passant devant une chambre. Est-ce quelque chose dont je dois être conscient ?

transcript.speaker: receptionist
transcript.text: I apologise for any inconvenience. Our maintenance team is already dealing with it. Your room has a brand-new unit, so you shouldn't have any issues.
transcript.translation: Je m'excuse pour tout inconvénient. Notre équipe de maintenance s'en occupe déjà. Votre chambre a une unité toute neuve, donc vous ne devriez pas avoir de problèmes.

transcript.speaker: guest
transcript.text: Great, that's reassuring. Thank you for all your help.
transcript.translation: Très bien, c'est rassurant. Merci de votre aide.

transcript.speaker: receptionist
transcript.text: My pleasure. Here is your key card. The lift is just around the corner. Enjoy your stay!
transcript.translation: Avec plaisir. Voici votre carte-clé. L'ascenseur est juste au coin. Bon séjour !

vocabulary.definition: An arrangement to have a room, seat, or table kept for your use.
vocabulary.example_sentence: I made a reservation for two nights online.

vocabulary.definition: To give someone a better room, seat, or service than they originally paid for.
vocabulary.example_sentence: The receptionist upgraded us to a suite for free.

vocabulary.definition: Given free of charge as a courtesy.
vocabulary.example_sentence: The hotel offers complimentary breakfast every morning.

vocabulary.definition: The work of keeping something in good condition.
vocabulary.example_sentence: The maintenance team fixed the broken lift quickly.

vocabulary.definition: A mild difficulty or problem causing slight trouble.
vocabulary.example_sentence: We apologise for any inconvenience during our renovation.

vocabulary.definition: A plastic card used like a key to open hotel room doors.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Swipe your key card against the door to enter.
